name: Prospero
creator: William Shakespeare
practice: Space transformation, theatrical magic, using an island as a stage
approach: The island IS the theater. Every natural feature — the cave, the shore, the grove — becomes a stage for transformation. Prospero's magic is really theatrical direction: he orchestrates encounters, creates spectacles, designs emotional arcs. Then he breaks his staff — the designer lets the space be itself again.
body_of_work: Transforms a wild island into a designed experience through which his enemies journey toward understanding. Creates the masque (layer 7 activation), orchestrates encounters (layer 5 social), and ultimately releases control (layer 10 catalyst — the space activates without him).
why_instructive: He's the sphere producer — someone who transforms a place through designed experience. His breaking of the staff at the end is the crucial insight: the activation must eventually sustain itself without the designer.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — theatrical magic as the ultimate creative practice

sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — using a place's history (exile, isolation) as material
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the island experience IS the activation
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — what remains when the designer withdraws
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — breaking the staff. The space continues without the artist.
